1. Only characters who have reached Level 40 may enter the Void Spire mode.

<TowerRule_18>2. This mode requires a Divine Tech Setup saved from the Purge Zone mode. When settling a run on any Standard mode map at Risk Level 5+ with any character, you can save the "choose 1 of 3" Tech obtained in that run as a Setup, and apply it to the Void Spire mode.</>

3. The Void Spire can be challenged repeatedly to earn corresponding rewards.

4. Clearing any layer of the Void Spire grants a chance to upgrade your Divine Tech.

<TowerRule_18>5. Every 5th layer (5, 10, 15, 20, etc.) in the Void Spire is a BOSS stage. Entering each BOSS stage consumes one Corrupted Crystal (deducted only upon success).</>

6. No EXP can be gained in the Void Spire mode.

1. By dismantling Mythic weapons and armor, you can extract Mythic Modules and install them in slots to gain the same effects.
2. Can install one Weapon Module and one Armor Module. Only supports weapon/armor types usable by the current character.
3. Installed Modules cannot be changed during combat.
<ModuleRule_14>4. If the Warwing and equipped equipment share the same Module, its effect does not stack.</>

Dear Specialists,

The brand-new Void Spire mode and its accompanying progression system are now available for experience. To be candid, this mode is still in its early stages, with limited content in terms of gameplay, map variety, and enemy types. Its overall completeness still needs improvement.

We are launching it now to allow interested players to try it out as early as possible and join us in the subsequent co-creation process. We promise to keep updating, continuously expand content, and refine the gameplay. Your feedback will be invaluable in helping us polish it further.

If you have high expectations for content completeness, we recommend exploring other game modes first and returning to the Void Spire once it has been further enhanced. Whether you choose to try it now or later, we sincerely appreciate your continued support!

Thank you for growing with us.

Contracts are consumable blueprint items. They come in various types and bind multiple pieces of equipment to provide additional set bonuses. Contracts have a chance to drop in Headhunt Assault mode and in Purge Zone at Risk Level 5 or above.

Pact Guide:
1. Consume the item - Divine Pact to add a Pact Set Effect to equipment.
2. When equipped items meet the slot and quantity requirements of the Pact Set, the corresponding set effect is activated.
Pacts must be used at the Artisan NPC to add set effects to multiple pieces of equipment.

Ver 0.8.1.7
Version Date: 2026.1.27

Update Notes:

Characters
  - Fixed an issue where the Rocket Launcher could not trigger Chase Tech.

Divine Tech
  - Fixed an issue where the Gunsmith faction could present an empty selection screen.
  - Fixed an issue that could soft-lock the game at the ""Select Divine Tech"" screen if players had banned all available Tech options.

Events
  - Adjusted the effects of the ""Equivalent Exchange"" event. Health Regen can no longer be reduced below 0.

Monsters
  - Optimized the visual effect for monster attack warning ranges.
  - Fixed an issue where certain monster visual effects were not displaying on low graphics settings.
  - Fixed an issue where elite monster visual effects could display incorrectly in specific situations.

UI
  - The on-screen prompt for toggling Auto-Fire (Q key) is now more prominent to help new players.

Missions
  - Fixed an issue where the completion criteria for some quests were not triggering correctly.

NPC
  - Fixed an issue where the mouse cursor could remain in ""Dismantle mode"" after closing the Artisan's Dismantle interface.

Settings
  - Added ""Screen Shake"" and ""Shake Intensity"" options.
  - Fixed an issue where the Frame Rate Limit could automatically reset to 60.

Stability
  - Fixed known issues that could cause game stuttering or unexpected crashes.

Other
  - Updated the game's launch icon.

Ver 0.8.1.8
Version Date: 2026.1.30

Update Notes:

Combat
  - Increased rewards (Credit, Character Career EXP, Equipment Drop Rarity) for Risk Levels 4 through 6.

Monsters
  - Reduced damage coefficient of [Burn] and [Poison] effects inflicted on players by all monsters.
  - Reduced Health for normal, elite, and Boss monsters in Risk Level 6.
  - BOSS: Corrupted Ancient
    - Fixed an issue where ground poison pool damage could stack abnormally.
  - BOSS: Sandstorm Lord
    - Reduced projectile speed in Risk Level 6.
  - BOSS: Plague Angel
    - Reduced base Health.
    - Optimized the warning range display for some attacks.
    - Fixed an issue where some visual effects could display incorrectly under specific conditions.

Equipment
  - Optimized the visual drop effects for equipment.

Store
  - Added a Sell All button to sell all equipment in your Inventory at once.

Warehouse
  - Added support for moving Equipment/Pacts across warehouse pages.

Settings
  - Added new options for Crosshair Customization.

Localization
  - Completed Russian and German translation texts.
  - Fixed an issue where the game language could be switched incorrectly on operating systems with unsupported locales.

Controller
  - Fixed an issue where interaction in level selection and in-game event interfaces required moving the cursor to a specific position.

Audio
  - Adjusted the volume of some sound effects.

Stability

Ver 0.8.3.4
Version Date: 2026.5.15

Dear Elite Specialists,
    We are currently focusing our efforts on the development of new maps and characters, which has slowed down our update frequency… orzzz. We fully understand your anticipation for fresh content, and we truly appreciate every Specialist’s patience and support during this time. Please rest assured that we will continue to work hard, constantly polish and improve the gameplay experience, and strive to deliver richer, higher‑quality content to you as soon as possible. Thank you again for your understanding and companionship!

Here are the details of this update:

New Equipment
  - Added 6 new Mythic equipment pieces
    - Venom Queen: [Tactical Skill – Venom Matrix] rotation speed greatly increased, and each Dash has a chance to spray venom in all directions dealing damage.
    - Sandstorm Grasp: Shooting an enemy fires a hook that pulls the target to you and deals [Psionic] damage.
    - Colossus Greaves: When taking damage, stomp the ground, releasing multiple shockwaves that deal [Psionic] damage.
    - Painful Shackles: Crit Chance increased, but suffer constant damage while Health is above a certain threshold.
    - Psionic  Armor: Damage taken is reduced while shielded.
    - Energy Core: Converts a fixed percentage of max Health into a shield, and when Health exceeds a certain threshold, all Health recovery is instead converted into a shield.

New Features
  - Reforge feature updated
    - You can now reforge one normal affix + one Legendary Module affix (on Legendary‑quality and above equipment) per item, helping you obtain more suitable gear.
    - The Credits and Basic Essence required for reforging have been reduced by 50%, making it easier to get the affixes you want.
  - Headhunt Assault mode completely revamped
    - You can now choose the specific Boss you wish to challenge.
    - Each Boss has exclusive Mythic drops, and targeting them in Headhunt Assault gives you a higher chance to obtain those drops.
  - Added Employee Handbook
    - Talk to Sona at the base entrance to view the Employee Handbook.
    - The Handbook provides explanations for various game functions, helping you better understand “Reincarnation Insurance Company”.
    - New players will automatically see the Employee Handbook when entering their first battle, providing combat guidance to help them quickly get started.

Bug Fixes & Optimizations
  - Removed the automatic grenade‑throwing effect from the Mythic equipment “Grenadier Mechanical Arm”.
  - Optimized the visual feedback when Claire’s and Katerina’s [Psionic] talent “Psionic Combo” triggers successfully, making it clearer whether the combo was activated.
  - Added support for custom key bindings for movement keys (W/A/S/D).
  - Optimized the performance and memory usage of projectile and bullet effects.
  - Fixed an issue where frozen monsters could be affected by external forces such as knockback.
  - Optimized Luna’s (bow) shooting animation.
  - Fixed an issue where holding down the fire button, opening any pause‑able interface, and then closing it would prevent further shooting.
  - Fixed an issue where the “revert talent point” prompt disappeared unexpectedly in the talent interface.
  - Fixed various text errors.
